english: (in Ts. by way of salutation); rgya-, bod-, sog-źwa (rgya-, bod-, sog zhwa) Chinese, Tibetan, Mongolian cap; dgun-źwa (dgun zhwa) winter-cap, dbyar-źwa (dbyar zhwa) summer-hat (light felt-hats adapted to the warmer season); phyiṅ-źwa (phying zhwa) hat or cap made of felt; wa-źwa (wa zhwa) cap made of the fur of a fox. — źwa-dkár, -nág, -dmár, -sér (zhwa dkar, -nag, -dmar, -ser) white, black, red, yellow cap, denoting occasionally also the wearers of such caps, esp. red-caps and yellow-caps, as belonging to different Lama-sects — źwa-khebs (zhwa khebs) the covering of a hat Cs. — źwa-tog (zhwa tog) top ornament of a hat Cs., prob. a button, v. tog (tog). — źwa-gźól (zhwa gzhol) brim, źwa-ri (zhwa ri) crown of a hat Cs., in Ld. however ri (ri) denotes the brim or flap. — Schl. p. 171 calls a low conical cap of the Chief Lama ṅa-t'on-źa (nga thon zha).

also rá-nye (ra nye) Cs., lead, źá-nyei rtiṅ-rdo (zha nyei rting rdo) sounding-lead, plummet Pth.; źa-nye-rdó (zha nye rdo) lead-ore Cs.; źá-nyei ćus sbyár-ba (zha nyei chus sbyar ba) to fill up (a groove or juncture) with molten lead Glr.; źá-nye dkár-po (zha nye dkar po) C., tin, also źa-dkár (zha dkar), gśa- (gsha-) or bśa-dkár (bsha dkar); źá-nye nág-po (zha nye nag po) lead, (Cs.'s 'white lead and black lead' seem to be a mere conjecture); źa-śóg (zha shog) (tin-foil Sch. (?)), thin plates of lead.

lame; lameness; gen. źá-bo (zha bo) lame, halting; a lame person, cripple, B. and col.; źá-mo (zha mo) fem.; *źá-wo ćo dug* (zha wo co dug) W. he is lame, he limps; rkáṅ- (rkang-) or lág-źa-ćan (lag zha can) having a lame foot or hand.
Glr. and vulgo, v. źál-ba (zhal ba).
cup, bowl, = phor-pa (phor pa), ko-re (ko re) Cs.
n. of the author of a little glossary, called Zamatog, źá-lu lo-tsa-ba (zha lu lo tsa ba) or lo-ćén (lo chen).

1. źág-pa (zhag pa) (only Schr., Cs.), *źág-po* (zhag po) W, *źág-ma* (zhag ma) Lt., W., resp. dguṅ-źág (dgung zhag) a day, the time from one sun-rise to another (cf. on the other hand nyí-ma (nyi ma) 2); źag ćig (zhag cig) a day, and adv : one day, once; *źag ćig-gi źág-la* (zhag cig gi zhag la) W. is also used of a future day: *źag ćig de dus leb yin* (zhag cig de dus leb yin) W. once the time will come; źag _ga-nas (zhag 'ga nas) after a few days Mil.; źag dú-ma lón-par (zhag du ma lon par) after many days Dzl.; ṅa di-riṅ ná-niṅ léb-źag* (nga di ring na ning leb zhag) W. this is the day of our arrival a year ago; *d_e-źag* (de zhag) W. lately, the other day, a short time ago; *dáṅ-źag* (dang zhag) W. yesterday; recently, *dáṅ-źag za-nyí-ma* (dang zhag za nyi ma) W. last sunday; *náṅ-źag* (nang zhag) W. some time ago, *náṅ-źag stón-ka* (nang zhag ston ka) W. last autumn; *kár-saṅ-źag* (kar sang zhag) W. the day before yesterday; źág-nas źág-tu (zhag nas zhag tu) from day to day; *źág-daṅ(-źag)* (zhag dang zhag) W. every day, always; źag bdun (zhag bdun) seven days, źag-bdun-phrág (zhag bdun phrag) a week, źag-bdun-phrág źe-brgyád (zhag bdun phrag zhe brgyad) forty-eight weeks Thgy. — źag-gráṅs (zhag grangs) the date, *źag-dáṅ gyáb-će* (zhag dang gyab ce) W. to date. — źag-mál (zhag mal) a station, day's journey, quarters Cs., źág-sa (zhag sa) id. Cs. — *źag-záṅ* (zhag zang) W. holiday. — 2. fat, grease, in a liquid state, = tsil-ḱu (tsil khu) S.g.; also melted and congealed again W.; fig. the fat of the country, fertility, yúl-la źag med (yul la zhag med) the country is barren Ma.; źág-ćan (zhag can) greasy, oily, źag-méd (zhag med) lean; źag-phór (zhag phor) a cup, vessel, for grease Cs. — 3. fog, smoke, dry vapour, filling the atmosphere in autumn.

leash, rope with a noose, e.g. for catching wild horses, źags-tág (zhags thag) Cs, źags-dbyúg (zhags dbyug) Sch. id., rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) Cs., ṕen pa (phen pa) Sch. to throw the noose; źags-pas, źags-tag-gis _dzin-pa (zhags pas, zhags thag gis 'dzin pa) frq. fig., as Schl. 213; sbrul-źágs (sbrul zhags) noose consisting of a serpent, for catching any hurtful creature Glr.; frq. as an attribute of the gods.

vulgo á-źaṅ (a zhang), uncle by the mother's side, mother's brother; źaṅ-brgyúd (zhang brgyud) his offspring Cs.; źaṅ-nyén (zhang nyen) in a gen. sense, relations by the mother's side Dom.; źaṅ-tsá (zhang tsha) sister's son. — tsa-źáṅ (tsha zhang), resp. dbon-źáṅ (dbon zhang) 1. nephew and uncle, by the mother's side, also applied to spiritual brotherhood Mil. — 2. son-in-law and brother-in-law Glr.
Glr. seems to be a kind of title given to a minister (or magistrate).
ancient n. of the province of Guge Glr.
weak, feeble, frq., the opp. to drág-po (drag po); ṅa jí-ltar źan yaṅ (nga ji ltar zhan yang) as weak, as miserable as I am (says a cripple) Pth.;
